title: The Macro Minute: It is time to take some chips off the AI table?

Happy Wednesday out there. Team 42, it's your escape artist here at Dairus Delta to present our macro minute for Wednesday, June 3rd, 2026. As always, let's start with the executive summary from today's lead-off morning note. So, let's dive right in. Today's key macro question is, is it time to take some chips off the AI table? The short answer is, from a short-term trade perspective, yes. If you're a medium to long-term investor, however, no. All dips in this AI bubble should be bought until our global macro risk matrix signals a transition to a risk-off market regime. Which our systematic risk management overlays, Kiss and Dr. Mo, are engineered to respond to in real time. In terms of analytical nuance, yesterday's bearish signal in mega-cap growth from our crowding model front-ran today's pullback and coincides with a bevy of key high-frequency economic data that confirms several dynamics. Number one, the resilient US economy remains resilient. Number two, our paradigm, c.a.k.a. run-it-hot theme, is gaining steam, which means the US business cycle is gaining steam. Number three, the buildup of core inflationary pressures in the US economy persists. And number four, among those core inflationary pressures is a deepening mismatch between labor supply and labor demand. Two critical known unknowns are, number one, how will Fed Chair Wash react to signals from the data and the market, according to our market-implied Fed neutral rate model, that the Fed is falling behind the curve in an increasingly dovish manner? And number two, how will Wash's colleagues on the FOMC react to his guidance given his legacy of criticism regarding their performance? These are human beings. It's impossible to know the answers to these critical questions at the current juncture, which means it's impossible to determine the near-term path of asset markets at the current juncture. Anyone who says they know for sure is full of poop and has not done enough research to confirm their thesis. All told, we believe any volatility this summer is likely to be resolved in a positive manner into and through year-end because it is likely that Kevin Warsh convinces his colleagues on the FOMC to look through the latest inflationary supply shock. This dynamic would likely propel the AI stock market bubble to new rationally exuberant heights. Notice I said rationally, not irrationally, like some of the bear porn providers want you to believe. All right, transition to our 42 macro dashboard as always grab up the question from our community. This one's not really a question but a comment. It touched my heart today so I felt like sharing. So, don't shoot the messenger. Says the first post is a note of thanks. Good day everyone miss and Mr. Dale. Says I've been a member for some months now but this is my first post. Given some of the post recently, I just wanted to add a note of my appreciation of 42 macro and KISS. Subscribing to 42 macro has improved my investment returns significantly and has been an education in financial markets in itself. And all this is at an important juncture in my life. I was made redundant a few months ago and simultaneously became an older father. The subscription give me confidence in my ability to provide financially into the future. This is exactly why we built our company to help every investor in the world gain financial freedom. I don't care if you're managing trillions of dollars in global fixed income money. We have several of those clients or if you are someone who just became a father. You know, what you had at a obviously a very difficult time in his life from a from a career standpoint. You know, we want to bridge that gap and and create as much positivity and financial freedom as we possibly can and so we're really grateful for each and every opportunity that we have to do that. We're grateful for each time you guys tune in to these macro minutes. So, we'll let you go. Terry Sandell signing off our macro minute for Wednesday, June 3rd, 2026. Best of luck out there today. We'll catch you back here tomorrow. Cheers. >> Mhm.
